hypothesis: Source7's tag should survive if original bad byte slot 7 is replaced only by spare byte placement slot 8.

source_change.description: Force source slot 7 to use write-side byte placement slot 8 in dfii_byte_phase_assoc_word5; otherwise preserve the v99/v103/v108 no-ODELAY init-clean path.

source6_baseline_restored.interpretation: Restores the known-good source6 shape after v108 moved source6 out of slot6.

source7_forced_through_spare_slot8.result: source7 still fails when only byte placement is changed to slot8

source8_control.interpretation: Spare slot8/source8 path itself is alive in its native source phase/index.

decision.conclusion: A simple byte placement remap from source7 to slot8 is not sufficient.
decision.classification: The missing lane is now constrained to the combination of source phase/index and byte placement, not only source tag identity and not only physical slot8 health.
decision.next_gate: Test a full source-index/phase remap: emit the logical source7 tag through the native source8 path, or compare generated DFI phase/word packing against the 9-byte LiteDRAM/YPCB grouping before native-port remapping.
